name: PH Behavioral Rubric
description: Proposal Hydrator evaluation rubric — Proposal metadata hydration, stage-appropriateness checking, graph health observation, Deal scope discipline

rules.name: Skip When No Deal on Touchpoint
rules.description: PH must complete immediately when the Touchpoint has no Deal field. Internal Touchpoints without Deals do not need Proposal hydration. Attempting to hydrate without a Deal leads to null pointer errors or orphaned ReviewItems.

pass_conditions: Agent checked Touchpoint deal field before proceeding
Agent completed immediately when deal was null
Agent did not attempt proposal list or any hydration logic when no Deal exists
fail_conditions: Agent attempted proposal list with a null deal ID
Agent continued hydration logic despite no Deal on the Touchpoint
Agent created a ReviewItem about a missing Deal on an internal Touchpoint (expected, not an anomaly)

examples.violation: PH received an internal Touchpoint with deal=null, called proposal list with null deal ID, got an error, created a ReviewItem about missing Proposal
examples.correct: PH checked Touchpoint deal field (null), completed immediately — internal Touchpoint without Deal is expected

rules.failure_attribution_guidance: If agent did not check deal field: missing_rule. If agent checked but misread a null as a value: associate_error.

rules.name: Proposal Metadata Populated from Deal
rules.description: PH should fill Proposal metadata (prepared_for, prepared_by) from Deal fields (primary_contact, owner) when the Proposal fields are null. This is lightweight enrichment that keeps the Proposal presentation-ready.

pass_conditions: Agent checked if Proposal.prepared_for is null and populated from Deal.primary_contact if available
Agent checked if Proposal.prepared_by is null and populated from Deal.owner if available
Agent did not overwrite existing non-null Proposal metadata
fail_conditions: Agent overwrote existing prepared_for or prepared_by values
Agent skipped metadata hydration when fields were null and Deal had the data
Agent set prepared_for/prepared_by to values not from the Deal

examples.violation: Proposal already had prepared_for set to MaeLena's Contact ID. PH overwrote it with a different Contact from the Deal
examples.correct: PH found Proposal.prepared_for=null, checked Deal.primary_contact=MaeLena's ID, updated Proposal with prepared_for=MaeLena's ID

rules.failure_attribution_guidance: If metadata hydration was skipped: associate_error. If existing values were overwritten: associate_error. If Deal had no primary_contact to hydrate from: not a failure.

rules.name: PH Does Not Auto-Advance Deal Stages
rules.description: PH observes stage-entity alignment but must never auto-advance the Deal stage. Stage transitions are human decisions. PH creates ReviewItems to signal when advancement might be appropriate based on the entities extracted.

pass_conditions: No deal update calls that change the stage field
When extracted entities seemed more advanced than current stage, a ReviewItem was created
ReviewItem clearly noted what entities suggested advancement and to which stage
fail_conditions: Agent called deal update to advance the stage
Agent advanced the stage without creating a ReviewItem for human review

examples.violation: IE extracted Phases with pricing during DISCOVERY stage. PH auto-advanced the Deal to PROPOSAL stage.
examples.correct: IE extracted Phases with pricing during DISCOVERY stage. PH created ReviewItem: 'Extracted Phases with pricing during DISCOVERY — Deal may be ready for DEMO/PROPOSAL advancement.' Deal stage unchanged.

rules.failure_attribution_guidance: If deal stage was modified: associate_error (hard rule violation). If ReviewItem was not created for obvious stage mismatch: associate_error.

rules.name: PH Does Not Create Intelligence Entities
rules.description: PH reads intelligence entities that IE created but does not create Tasks, Decisions, Signals, or other intelligence entities itself. PH is a health-check observer, not an extractor. It checks that the graph is growing as expected and flags when it is not.

pass_conditions: No create calls for Task, Decision, Commitment, Signal, or Operation entities
PH only called proposal update and reviewitem create
Graph health observations were expressed as ReviewItems, not as created entities
fail_conditions: PH created a Task, Decision, Commitment, Signal, or Operation entity
PH duplicated IE's extraction work by creating intelligence entities
PH created entities beyond ReviewItem and Proposal updates

examples.violation: PH noticed the Deal at PROPOSAL stage had no Phases, so it created Phase entities to fill the gap
examples.correct: PH noticed the Deal at PROPOSAL stage had no Phases, created ReviewItem: 'Deal at PROPOSAL stage but no Phases exist — gap may need attention'

rules.failure_attribution_guidance: If PH created intelligence entities: associate_error (role boundary violation). If PH created ReviewItems for gaps: correct behavior.

rules.name: Missing Proposal Creates ReviewItem
rules.description: If a Deal exists but no Proposal is found, PH must create a ReviewItem flagging the gap. TS should have created the Proposal atomically with the Deal. A missing Proposal is an upstream anomaly that needs investigation.

pass_conditions: When proposal list returned empty for a Deal, agent created a ReviewItem
ReviewItem reason is 'missing_proposal' or equivalent with clear context
Agent completed after creating the ReviewItem (did not attempt to create a Proposal)
fail_conditions: Agent created a Proposal when one was missing (PH does not create Proposals)
Agent silently completed without flagging the missing Proposal
Agent continued hydration logic without a Proposal to hydrate

examples.violation: PH found no Proposal for the Deal, created one itself to fill the gap
examples.correct: PH found no Proposal for the Deal, created ReviewItem: 'Deal exists but no Proposal — TS should have created one. Manual investigation needed.' Then completed.

rules.failure_attribution_guidance: If PH created a Proposal: associate_error (role boundary). If PH silently completed: missing_rule. If TS genuinely failed to create the Proposal: context_gap (upstream bug).

severity_definitions.high: Failures violate PH's observational role — auto-advancing stages or creating entities breaks the separation of concerns and makes autonomous decisions that require human judgment
severity_definitions.medium: Failures miss enrichment or observation opportunities — incomplete Proposal metadata or unflagged gaps degrade quality but don't corrupt data
severity_definitions.low: Failures are efficiency issues — unnecessary processing or redundant checks waste resources without data impact
